title: Client is Send but not Sync; justification is in a safety comment
description: client.rs implements unsafe impl Send for Client with a safety comment explaining that the C context is not thread-safe for concurrent access but safe to transfer ownership across threads (no thread-local state). !Sync is the default because Client contains raw pointers. This is the correct threading model: Send allows using Client inside a Mutex or passing to spawn_blocking; !Sync prevents simultaneous access from multiple threads. A future contributor might try to add Sync to avoid mutex overhead, but the C comm layer is explicitly documented as not thread-safe. The Send-but-not-Sync split is a well-established Rust pattern for FFI types with exclusive-access semantics.
